definition: Labor market data is subject to revision risk that analysts systematically underweight. BLS nonfarm payrolls 90% CI is ±130,000 jobs. The March 2024 benchmark revision revised cumulative 2023 job gains down by ~818,000 (~68,000/month). JOLTS response rates have fallen from ~60% to ~30%. A +150,000 payroll print is not statistically distinguishable from 0 or +300,000.
evidence: March 2024 benchmark revision: cumulative 2023 job gains revised down ~818,000
JOLTS survey response rates fallen from ~60% to ~30%
pair_3 debate called this 'the single most practically actionable insight across both analyses'
open_questions: Are alternative real-time labor market indicators (ADP, Indeed postings, UI claims) more reliable?
Should JOLTS data be formally downweighted given collapsing response rates?
How should portfolio construction account for the noise in labor data?
